body:
## Bug

The Gemini embedding path in the memory search system lacks retry/backoff handling that the OpenAI and Voyage paths already have.

### What happens
- **OpenAI and Voyage embedding paths:** wrapped in retryAsync with shouldRetry for 429/5xx - 3 attempts, exponential backoff (300ms to 2s), jitter.
- **Gemini embedding path (submitGeminiBatch):** raw fetch with NO retry wrapper. If Gemini returns 429, it throws immediately. No backoff, no retry.
- Same for fetchGeminiBatchStatus and fetchGeminiFileContent - no retry wrappers either.

### Impact
When Gemini hits its daily quota (429), the errors throw unhandled. Since the memory system retries embedding on every session start/heartbeat, this spams 429s until the gateway becomes unstable.

### Expected behavior
Gemini embedding calls should be wrapped in the same retryAsync with exponential backoff that OpenAI and Voyage already use.

### Workaround
Switch embedding provider to OpenAI (text-embedding-3-small) which already has proper 429 handling.

### Version
2026.2.9

title: Gemini embedding path missing retry/backoff for 429 errors
